title: Ctenitis pansamalensis
taxonomy: Ctenitis pansamalensis is classified in the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or shield ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Ctenitis, a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The species Ctenitis pansamalensis is further divided into various subspecies, each with distinct characteristics and distribution patterns. Ctenitis pansamalensis is a member of the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. The classification of Ctenitis pansamalensis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns.

intro: Ctenitis pansamalensis is a species of fern native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis pansamalensis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
